title: Windows Update Installed During Enrollment
description: Informational: a Windows quality/cumulative update installed during the OOBE / ESP enrollment window (WindowsUpdateClient EventID 19). Surfaces updates applied mid-enrollment — useful context when correlating enrollment duration, reboots, or later issues with a specific KB.
explanation: One or more Windows updates installed during enrollment (e.g. **{{updateTitle}}**). This is normal when Windows Update runs during OOBE, but it extends enrollment time and can trigger a reboot mid-ESP. Track it when correlating enrollment duration or reboot count with a specific cumulative update, or when a device behaves differently after a particular KB landed during provisioning.

remediation.title: Optional review
steps: No action required for a successful install — this is informational.
If enrollment time or reboot count is a concern, review whether quality updates during OOBE are desired for your Autopilot profile.
Correlate the installed KB with any post-enrollment issues if the device behaves unexpectedly.
